items.title: Model selection via validation curves
items.description: Plotting train vs validation error against hyperparameters (tree depth, polynomial degree, network width) reveals when bias dominates (both errors high) vs when variance dominates (train–val gap). Teams pick complexity where validation error is minimized.

items.title: Feature engineering to reduce bias
items.description: Adding interaction terms, log transforms, or domain-specific features expands what a fixed model family can represent — lowering bias without switching algorithms (e.g. linear model + polynomial features).

items.title: Baseline model benchmarking
items.description: A constant or linear baseline with high error on train and test signals that the problem needs richer structure — high bias baselines set the floor before investing in deep models.

items.title: Regularization tuning in production ML
items.description: Ridge/L1 strength, dropout rate, and early stopping deliberately increase bias to control variance. Production pipelines grid-search these knobs using validation bias–variance tradeoffs.

items.title: Neural architecture scaling laws
items.description: Under-parameterized networks underfit (high bias); scaling width/depth reduces bias on hard tasks until data or optimization limits kick in — architecture search targets the bias floor for a dataset.
